restaurant_name: Mister O1 Extraordinary Pizza Sandy Springs
blurb:
This is hands down the best pizza in Sandy Springs and probably all of north Atlanta. It's actual Italian-style thin crust that's crispy and light, not the heavy stuff most places do. Great for date night or when you want pizza but don't want to feel terrible afterward.

They use ingredients from Italy and local sources, and you can taste the difference. The crust has this perfect char from their ovens and the toppings are really fresh. Even the salads are worth getting, which I never say about pizza places. My wife always orders one of their antipasti plates to start and it's never disappointed.

It gets busy on weekends so expect to wait a bit, but it's worth it. The 4.9 rating with almost 5,000 reviews isn't an accident.
address: 6405 Blue Stone Rd Ste 240, Atlanta, GA 30328, USA
hours: Monday: 11:30 AM – 9:30 PM, Tuesday: 11:30 AM – 9:30 PM, Wednesday: 11:30 AM – 9:30 PM, Thursday: 11:30 AM – 9:30 PM, Friday: 11:30 AM – 11:00 PM, Saturday: 11:30 AM – 11:00 PM, Sunday: 11:30 AM – 9:30 PM

blurb:
This is where we go when we want really good Indian food without the fancy restaurant prices. It's in the Perimeter area and feels like eating at someone's house with the family-style service and bright, casual vibe. Perfect for when you want to feed a group or try a bunch of different dishes.

The biryani is obviously their thing and it's worth ordering. They bring everything out on big platters meant for sharing, which works great if you're with friends or family who like to try each other's food. The portions are generous so you'll definitely have leftovers for tomorrow's lunch.

Just know they close that gap between lunch and dinner on weekdays, so don't show up at 3pm expecting to eat. Weekend hours are more flexible if you're looking for a late breakfast or early dinner.
address: 130 Perimeter Center Pl, Dunwoody, GA 30346, USA
hours: Monday: 11:30 AM – 9:30 PM, Tuesday: 11:30 AM – 9:30 PM, Wednesday: 11:30 AM – 9:30 PM, Thursday: 11:30 AM – 9:30 PM, Friday: 11:30 AM – 11:30 PM, Saturday: 9:00 AM – 11:30 PM, Sunday: 9:00 AM – 9:30 PM

blurb:
This is the real deal for French pastries and bread in Marietta. The owner is actually French, and you can tell the difference in everything they make. Perfect for weekend brunch or when you want to grab fresh baguettes and croissants. It feels like a little piece of France tucked into a strip mall.

The croissants are flaky and buttery like they should be, and the quiches are excellent for lunch. They also make beautiful little petits fours if you need something for a party. The coffee is good too, which isn't always a given at bakeries. I go there Saturday mornings when I want to feel fancy without driving to Buckhead.

Just know they close at 3:30 on Sundays and earlier than most places during the week. It's worth planning around because this kind of quality is hard to find in the suburbs.
address: 277 South Marietta Pkwy SW, Marietta, GA 30064, USA
hours: Mon-Sat 7:00 AM – 6:00 PM, Sun 9:00 AM – 3:30 PM

blurb:
This place absolutely lives up to all the hype you've heard about it. The atmosphere hits that perfect balance of lively but not too loud, and the service is genuinely helpful instead of just going through the motions. Good for friends' night out, casual dates, or really any time you want tacos that aren't just okay.

Start with the Esquite because it's packed with flavor and gets the meal off to a good start. The drink pitchers are perfect for sharing, especially the Pineapple Guava and Passion Fruit ones that somehow balance the sweetness and alcohol just right. The tacos themselves are what you came for and they don't disappoint.

They're open until midnight most nights and until 11pm on Sundays, which makes them perfect for those times when you want good food but it's past normal dinner hours.
address: 227 Sandy Springs Pl NE #506, Sandy Springs, GA 30328, USA
hours: Monday: 11:00 AM – 12:00 AM, Tuesday: 11:00 AM – 12:00 AM, Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 12:00 AM, Thursday: 11:00 AM – 12:00 AM, Friday: 11:00 AM – 12:00 AM, Saturday: 11:00 AM – 12:00 AM, Sunday: 11:00 AM – 11:00 PM

blurb:
This is the place you go when you need comfort food at 2am or want a proper breakfast at any hour. It's a classic neon-lit diner that's been feeding Marietta for decades, and being open 24/7 makes it perfect for shift workers, insomniacs, or anyone craving pancakes at midnight. The atmosphere is exactly what you'd expect from a real diner.

The menu is absolutely massive, but the breakfast items are what they do best. The pancakes are fluffy and huge, and they also do solid Greek dishes since the owners are Greek. My family goes here after late movies or when we want a big breakfast without waiting in line at some trendy brunch place.

Just know it gets busy during normal meal times, especially weekends. But that's part of the charm, and the staff keeps everything moving.
address: 306 Cobb Pkwy SE, Marietta, GA 30060, USA

blurb:
This is one of those places that makes it onto everyone's top dinner spots in Atlanta once they try it. Perfect for girls' night out or any time you want to try something different that's still approachable. The vibe is upscale but comfortable, and the service actually knows their stuff when you ask questions.

The hummus is a good way to start, and the chicken shish kabobs with that rice with lentils and raisins are what most people end up ordering. Their cocktails are really well done, not just an afterthought like at a lot of ethnic restaurants. Save room for the tiramisu because it's worth the extra few minutes at the table.

They do that break between lunch and dinner every day, so don't show up at 3pm expecting to eat. Weekend hours start earlier if you want a late lunch that turns into early dinner.
address: 6500 Aria Blvd Suite 500, Sandy Springs, GA 30328, USA

blurb:
If you've never done Brazilian all-you-can-eat, this is a great place to try it. They bring different grilled meats to your table on skewers, and you flip a little card to green when you want more or red when you need a break. It's fun for families and definitely fills you up. Good for celebrating something or when you just want to eat a lot of really good meat.

The cheese bread they bring out first is addictive, and the grilled pineapple is surprisingly good. They also have a salad bar if you want to pace yourself, but honestly most people come here for the barbecue. The servers are good about explaining everything if it's your first time.

Just come hungry because you're paying for the full experience whether you eat a little or a lot. Weekend evenings get busy, so call ahead if you have a big group.
address: 1275 Powers Ferry Rd ste 230, Marietta, GA 30067, USA
hours: Mon-Thu 10:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Fri-Sat 10:00 AM – 11:00 PM, Sun 10:00 AM – 10:00 PM

blurb:
This is basically a fancy food court right on the square, which is perfect when your group can't agree on what to eat. There are about twenty different vendors inside, so you can get tacos while your friend gets sushi and someone else gets barbecue. Great for families with picky eaters or when you're walking around the square and want options.

The setup is nice and clean, and most of the food is pretty good quality for food hall style. I usually end up at the taco place or the Asian spot, but there's pizza, burgers, and even some healthier options. You order at each vendor and then find a table in the common area.

Parking can be tricky during busy times since it's right downtown, but if you're already walking around the square anyway, it's convenient. Good for a casual lunch or dinner when you want to eat local without committing to one specific restaurant.
address: 68 North Marietta Pkwy NW, Marietta, GA 30060, USA
hours: Mon-Thu 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM, Fri-Sat 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Sun 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM

blurb:
This is the place for special occasions when you want to impress someone or celebrate something big. Right on the Chattahoochee with huge windows overlooking the water, it feels fancy without being stuffy. Good for anniversaries, client dinners, or when your parents visit and want somewhere nice.

The seafood is what they do best, and the Southern touches make it feel like Atlanta rather than just another expensive restaurant. The setting really makes the meal, especially if you can get a table by the windows at sunset. Service is the kind where they actually know what they're talking about when you ask questions.

Expect to spend some money here and make a reservation well ahead if it's a weekend. Sunday hours are weird with that break between brunch and dinner, so double check if you're planning something specific.
address: 6700 Powers Ferry Rd NW, Sandy Springs, GA 30339, USA
hours: Monday: 11:30 AM – 10:00 PM, Tuesday: 11:30 AM – 10:00 PM, Wednesday: 11:30 AM – 10:00 PM, Thursday: 11:30 AM – 10:00 PM, Friday: 11:30 AM – 11:00 PM, Saturday: 12:00 – 11:00 PM, Sunday: 11:00 AM – 4:00 PM, 5:00 – 9:00 PM

blurb:
This is where I go when I want really good Middle Eastern food without paying downtown prices. It's in a strip mall off Franklin Gateway, but don't let that fool you. The food is authentic and the portions are generous. Perfect for a casual lunch or when you want to try something different for dinner.

The shawarma is what most people order, and it's excellent. The falafel is crispy outside and fluffy inside, and they make their own pita bread fresh. They also have good vegetarian options if that's what you need. Everything comes with this great garlic sauce that I could probably eat with a spoon.

Prices are really reasonable, especially for how much food you get. It's become our go-to spot when we want something healthy but filling.
address: 1175 Franklin Gateway SE, Marietta, GA 30067, USA
